an1.100:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great harm as this, *bhikkhus*: laziness.
an1.101:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great benefit as this, *bhikkhus*: the arousal of energy.
an1.101:1.2: The arousal of energy, *bhikkhus*, leads to great benefit."
an1.102-109:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great harm as this, *bhikkhus*: great desire … having few desires … discontent … contentment … inappropriate attention [*ayoniso manasikāra*] … appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] … lack of full awareness [*asampajaññaṁ*] … full awareness [*sampajaññaṁ*] … the twelfth.
an1.110:1.1: "Taking external factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great harm as this, *bhikkhus*: bad friendship.
an1.110:1.2: Bad friendship, *bhikkhus*, leads to great harm."
an1.111:1.1: "Taking external factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great benefit as this, *bhikkhus*: good friendship.
an1.111:1.2: Good friendship, *bhikkhus*, leads to great benefit."
an1.112:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great harm as this, *bhikkhus*: the pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of wholesome *dhammas*.
an1.112:1.2: The pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of wholesome *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, leads to great harm."
an1.113:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great benefit as this, *bhikkhus*: the pursuit of wholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas*.
an1.113:1.2: The pursuit of wholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, leads to great benefit."
an1.114:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: heedlessness.
an1.114:1.2: Heedlessness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.115:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: heedfulness.
an1.115:1.2: Heedfulness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.116:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: laziness.
an1.116:1.2: Laziness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.117:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: the arousal of energy.
an1.117:1.2: The arousal of energy, *bhikkhus*, leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.118-128:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: great desire … having few desires … discontent … contentment … inappropriate attention [*ayoniso manasikāra*] … appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] … lack of full awareness [*asampajaññaṁ*] … full awareness [*sampajaññaṁ*] … bad friendship … good friendship … the pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of wholesome *dhammas*.
an1.118-128:1.2: The pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of wholesome *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.129:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, even a single thing that leads so much to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma* as this, *bhikkhus*: the pursuit of wholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas*.
an1.129:1.2: The pursuit of wholesome *dhammas* and the non-pursuit of unwholesome *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *dhamma*."
an1.130:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is not *dhamma* as *dhamma* — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the harm, the hurt, and the *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.130:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.131:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is *dhamma* as not *dhamma* — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the harm, the hurt, and the *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.131:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.132-139:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is not *vinaya* as *vinaya* … who present what is *vinaya* as not *vinaya* … who present what was not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata as spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata … who present what was spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata as not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata … who present what was not practiced by the Tathāgata as practiced by the Tathāgata … who present what was practiced by the Tathāgata as not practiced by the Tathāgata … who present what was not laid down by the Tathāgata as laid down by the Tathāgata … who present what was laid down by the Tathāgata as not laid down by the Tathāgata;
an1.132-139:1.2: those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the harm, the hurt, and the *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.132-139:1.3: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit, and they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.132-139:1.5: The tenth chapter, the Second Chapter on Heedlessness and So On, is complete.
an1.98:0.2: 10. The Second Chapter on Heedlessness and So On
an1.98:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great harm as this, *bhikkhus*: heedlessness.
an1.99:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I do not see even a single factor that leads to such great benefit as this, *bhikkhus*: heedfulness.
